deployments.name: trustyai-service-operator-controller-manager
issues: no PodDisruptionBudget configured
no HorizontalPodAutoscaler configured
Unpinned base image: registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:latest
Unpinned base image: registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi-minimal:latest
Unpinned base image: registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi-minimal:latest
Unpinned base image: rust-builder
Unpinned base image: fms-guardrails-orchestr8-builder
Unpinned base image: fms-guardrails-orchestr8-builder
Unpinned base image: fms-guardrails-orchestr8-builder
No cache configuration: all informers are cluster-wide (OOM risk). See https://book.kubebuilder.io/reference/watching-resources/filtering for cache filtering patterns
Type ConfigMap is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type Deployment is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type EvalHub is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type GuardrailsOrchestrator is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type InferenceService is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type Job is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type LMEvalJob is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type Namespace is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type NemoGuardrails is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type Service is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type TrustyAIService is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)

api_types.name: EvalHubStatus
api_types.doc: EvalHubStatus defines the observed state of EvalHub

fields.name: Phase
fields.doc: Current phase of the EvalHub (Pending, Ready, Error)

fields.name: Replicas
fields.doc: Number of desired replicas

fields.name: ReadyReplicas
fields.doc: Number of ready replicas

fields.name: Conditions
fields.doc: Conditions represent the latest available observations

fields.name: Ready
fields.doc: Ready indicates whether the EvalHub is ready to serve requests

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: URL where the EvalHub service is accessible

fields.name: ActiveProviders
fields.doc: List of active providers

fields.name: ActiveCollections
fields.doc: List of active collections

fields.name: LastUpdateTime
fields.doc: Last time the status was updated

api_types.name: EvalHubList
api_types.doc: EvalHubList contains a list of EvalHub

api_types.name: EvalHub
api_types.doc: EvalHub is the Schema for the evalhubs API

api_types.name: DatabaseSpec
api_types.doc: DatabaseSpec defines database configuration for EvalHub

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the database backend. Must be explicitly set.

fields.name: Secret
fields.doc: Name of the K8s Secret containing a pre-composed db-url key. Required when type is "postgresql"; ignored for "sqlite".

fields.name: MaxOpenConns
fields.doc: Maximum number of open database connections (postgresql only)

fields.name: MaxIdleConns
fields.doc: Maximum number of idle database connections (postgresql only)

api_types.name: OTELSpec
api_types.doc: OTELSpec defines OpenTelemetry configuration for EvalHub. Its presence in the CR spec implies OTEL is enabled.

fields.name: SamplingRatio
fields.doc: Trace sampling ratio as a string-encoded float between "0" and "1" (e.g. "0.5"). Defaults to "1.0" (sample everything) when omitted.

api_types.name: EvalHubSpec
api_types.doc: EvalHubSpec defines the desired state of EvalHub

fields.name: Replicas
fields.doc: Number of replicas for the eval-hub deployment

fields.name: Env
fields.doc: Environment variables for the eval-hub container

fields.name: Providers
fields.doc: Providers is the list of OOTB provider names to mount into the deployment. Each name must match a provider-name label on a ConfigMap in the operator namespace.

fields.name: Collections
fields.doc: Collections is the list of OOTB collection names to mount into the deployment. Each name must match a collection-name label on a ConfigMap in the operator namespace.

fields.name: Database
fields.doc: Database configuration for persistent storage. This field is required: the operator will not start the service without an explicit database configuration. Set type to "postgresql" with a secret reference, or "sqlite" for lightweight/development deployments.

fields.name: Otel
fields.doc: OpenTelemetry configuration for observability. When set, the operator includes OTEL settings in the generated config. When omitted, the service uses its defaults (OTEL disabled).

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: e.g. "generator" or "detector"

fields.name: Scheme
fields.doc: e.g., "http" or "https"

fields.name: Conditions
fields.doc: Conditions describes the state of the GuardrailsOrchestrator resource.

fields.name: AutoConfigState
fields.doc: AutoConfigState describes information about the generated autoconfiguration

api_types.name: GuardrailsOrchestrator
api_types.doc: GuardrailsOrchestrator is the Schema for the guardrailsorchestrators API.

api_types.name: GuardrailsOrchestratorList
api_types.doc: GuardrailsOrchestratorList contains a list of GuardrailsOrchestrator.

fields.name: InferenceServiceToGuardrail
fields.doc: The name of the inference service that provides the vLLM generation model to-be-guardrailed

fields.name: DetectorServiceLabelToMatch
fields.doc:
/* Label key to use when automatically identifying guardrail detector inference services.
	If provided, all inference services with the label `$detectorServiceLabelToMatch: true` will be used as a guardrails detector.
	If not provided, the default match label is `trustyai/guardrails`, and the autoconfig will use all inference services with the label `trustyai/guardrails: true` as detectors.
	*/

api_types.name: GuardrailsOrchestratorSpec
api_types.doc: GuardrailsOrchestratorSpec defines the desired state of GuardrailsOrchestrator.

fields.name: Replicas
fields.doc: INSERT ADDITIONAL SPEC FIELDS - desired state of cluster Important: Run "make" to regenerate code after modifying this file Number of replicas

fields.name: OrchestratorConfig
fields.doc: Name of configmap containing generator, detector, and chunker arguments

fields.name: AutoConfig
fields.doc: Settings governing the automatic configuration of the orchestrator. Replaces `OrchestratorConfig`.

fields.name: EnableBuiltInDetectors
fields.doc: Boolean flag to enable/disable built-in detectors

fields.name: CustomDetectorsConfig
fields.doc: Name of configmap containing user-defined Python detectors. This is only used if EnableBuiltInDetectors is true

fields.name: EnableGuardrailsGateway
fields.doc: Boolean flag to enable/disable the guardrails sidecar gateway

fields.name: SidecarGatewayConfig
fields.doc: Name of the configmap containing guadrails sidecar gateway arguments

fields.name: OTelExporter
fields.doc: List of orchestrator enviroment variables for configuring the OTLP exporter

fields.name: LogLevel
fields.doc: Set log level in the orchestrator deployment

fields.name: TLSSecrets
fields.doc: Define TLS secrets to be mounted to the orchestrator. Secrets will be mounted at /etc/tls/$SECRET_NAME

fields.name: EnvVars
fields.doc: Define environment variables. These will be added to the orchestrator, gateway, and built-in detector pods.

fields.name: DisableOrchestrator
fields.doc: Boolean flag to disable the orchestrator container, if running the built-in detectors in standalone mode

api_types.name: OTelExporter
api_types.doc: OTelExporter defines the environment variables for configuring the OTLP exporter.

fields.name: OTLPProtocol
fields.doc: Sets the protocol for both traces and metrics

fields.name: OTLPTracesEndpoint
fields.doc: Overrides the OTLP endpoint for traces

fields.name: OTLPMetricsEndpoint
fields.doc: Overrides the OTLP endpoint for metrics

fields.name: EnableTraces
fields.doc: Specifies whether to enable tracing data export

fields.name: EnableMetrics
fields.doc: Specifies whether to enable metrics data export

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Unitxt metric id

fields.name: Ref
fields.doc: The name of the custom metric in the custom field. Its value is a JSON string for a custom Unitxt metric. Use the documentation here: https://www.unitxt.ai/en/latest/docs/adding_metric.html#adding-a-new-instance-metric to compose a custom metric, store it as a JSON file by calling the add_to_catalog API: https://www.unitxt.ai/en/latest/docs/saving_and_loading_from_catalog.html#adding-assets-to-the-catalog, and use the JSON content as the value here.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Unitxt task id

fields.name: Ref
fields.doc: The name of the custom task in the custom field. Its value is a JSON string for a custom Unitxt task. Use the documentation here: https://www.unitxt.ai/en/latest/docs/adding_task.html to compose a custom task, store it as a JSON file by calling the add_to_catalog API: https://www.unitxt.ai/en/latest/docs/saving_and_loading_from_catalog.html#adding-assets-to-the-catalog, and use the JSON content as the value here.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name of the custom artifact

fields.name: Value
fields.doc: Value of the custom artifact. It could be a JSON string or plain text depending on the artifact type

fields.name: Templates
fields.doc: The Unitxt custom templates

fields.name: SystemPrompts
fields.doc: The Unitxt custom System Prompts

fields.name: Metrics
fields.doc: The Unitxt custom metrics

fields.name: Tasks
fields.doc: The Unitxt custom tasks

api_types.name: TaskRecipe
api_types.doc: Use a task recipe to form a custom task. It maps to the Unitxt Recipe Find details of the Unitxt Recipe here: https://www.unitxt.ai/en/latest/unitxt.standard.html#unitxt.standard.StandardRecipe

fields.name: Card
fields.doc: The Unitxt dataset card

fields.name: Template
fields.doc: The Unitxt template

fields.name: SystemPrompt
fields.doc: The Unitxt System Prompt

fields.name: Task
fields.doc: The Unitxt Task

fields.name: Metrics
fields.doc: Metrics

fields.name: Format
fields.doc: The Unitxt format

fields.name: LoaderLimit
fields.doc: A limit number of records to load

fields.name: NumDemos
fields.doc: Number of fewshot

fields.name: DemosPoolSize
fields.doc: The pool size for the fewshot

api_types.name: GitSource
api_types.doc: GitSource specifies the git location of external tasks

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: URL specifies the git repository URL

fields.name: Branch
fields.doc: Branch specifies the git branch to use

fields.name: Commit
fields.doc: Commit specifies the git commit to use

fields.name: Path
fields.doc: Path specifies the path to the task file

api_types.name: CustomTaskSource
api_types.doc: CustomTaskSource specifies the source of custom tasks

fields.name: GitSource
fields.doc: GitSource specifies the git location of external tasks

api_types.name: CustomTasks
api_types.doc: CustomTasks specifies the custom (external) tasks to use

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Source specifies the source location of custom tasks

fields.name: TaskNames
fields.doc: TaskNames from lm-eval's task list and/or from custom tasks if CustomTasks is defined

fields.name: TaskRecipes
fields.doc: Task Recipes specifically for Unitxt

fields.name: CustomArtifacts
fields.doc: Custom Unitxt artifacts that can be used in a TaskRecipe

fields.name: CustomTasks
fields.doc: CustomTasks is a list of external tasks

fields.name: Env
fields.doc: Define Env information for the main container

fields.name: VolumeMounts
fields.doc: Define the volume mount information

fields.name: Resources
fields.doc: Compute Resources required by this container. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

fields.name: SecurityContext
fields.doc: SecurityContext defines the security options the container should be run with. If set, the fields of SecurityContext override the equivalent fields of PodSecurityContext. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

fields.name: PersistentVolumeClaimName
fields.doc: Use an existing PVC to store the outputs

fields.name: PersistentVolumeClaimManaged
fields.doc: Create an operator managed PVC

fields.name: OCISpec
fields.doc: Upload results to OCI registry

fields.name: Container
fields.doc: Extra container data for the lm-eval container

fields.name: Volumes
fields.doc: Specify the volumes information for the lm-eval and sidecar containers

fields.name: SideCars
fields.doc: Specify extra containers for the lm-eval job FIXME: aggregate the sidecar containers into the pod

fields.name: Affinity
fields.doc: If specified, the pod's scheduling constraints

fields.name: SecurityContext
fields.doc: SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes and common container settings. Optional: Defaults to empty.  See type description for default values of each field.

fields.name: Registry
fields.doc: Registry URL (e.g., quay.io, registry.redhat.com)

fields.name: Repository
fields.doc: Repository path (e.g., myorg/evaluation-results)

fields.name: Tag
fields.doc: Optional tag for the artifact (defaults to job name if not specified)

fields.name: Subject
fields.doc: Subject for the OCI artifact

fields.name: UsernameRef
fields.doc: Username for registry authentication

fields.name: PasswordRef
fields.doc: Password for registry authentication

fields.name: DockerConfigJsonRef
fields.doc: DockerConfigJson for registry authentication (alternative to username/password)

fields.name: VerifySSL
fields.doc: Whether to verify SSL certificates

api_types.name: OfflineStorageSpec
api_types.doc: OfflineStorageSpec defines the storage configuration for LMEvalJob's offline mode

api_types.name: OfflineSpec
api_types.doc: OfflineSpec defined the configuration for LMEvalJob's offline mode

api_types.name: ChatTemplate
api_types.doc: ChatTemplate defines the configuration for the applied chat template during the evaluation.

api_types.name: ProgressBar
api_types.doc: ProgressBar stores information about progress of various steps within an evaluation task.

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: The title of the step being performed

fields.name: Percent
fields.doc: The percent completion of this evaluation step

fields.name: ElapsedTime
fields.doc: The elapsed time that this step has been running

fields.name: RemainingTimeEstimate
fields.doc: The estimated time remaining for this evaluation step

fields.name: Count
fields.doc: Fractional progress through the evaluation step, presented as "$completed_items/$total_items"

api_types.name: LMEvalJobSpec
api_types.doc: LMEvalJobSpec defines the desired state of LMEvalJob

fields.name: Model
fields.doc: Model name

fields.name: ModelArgs
fields.doc: Args for the model

fields.name: TaskList
fields.doc: Evaluation task list

fields.name: NumFewShot
fields.doc: Sets the number of few-shot examples to place in context

fields.name: Limit
fields.doc: Accepts an integer, or a float between 0.0 and 1.0 . If passed, will limit the number of documents to evaluate to the first X documents (if an integer) per task or first X% of documents per task

fields.name: GenArgs
fields.doc: Map to `--gen_kwargs` parameter for the underlying library.

fields.name: LogSamples
fields.doc: If this flag is passed, then the model's outputs, and the text fed into the model, will be saved at per-document granularity

fields.name: BatchSize
fields.doc: Batch size for the evaluation. This is used by the models that run and are loaded locally and not apply for the commercial APIs.

fields.name: Pod
fields.doc: Specify extra information for the lm-eval job's pod

fields.name: Suspend
fields.doc: Suspend keeps the job but without pods. This is intended to be used by the Kueue integration

fields.name: Outputs
fields.doc: Outputs specifies storage for evaluation results

fields.name: Offline
fields.doc: Offline specifies settings for running LMEvalJobs in an offline mode

fields.name: AllowOnline
fields.doc: AllowOnly specifies whether the LMEvalJob can directly download remote code, datasets and metrics. Default is false.

fields.name: AllowCodeExecution
fields.doc: AllowCodeExecution specifies whether the LMEvalJob can execute remote code. Default is false.

fields.name: SystemInstruction
fields.doc: SystemInstruction will set the system instruction for all prompts passed to the evaluated model

fields.name: ChatTemplate
fields.doc: ChatTemplate defines whether to apply the default or specified chat template to prompts. This is required for chat-completions models.

api_types.name: LMEvalJobStatus
api_types.doc: LMEvalJobStatus defines the observed state of LMEvalJob

fields.name: PodName
fields.doc: The name of the Pod that runs the evaluation job

fields.name: State
fields.doc: State of the job

fields.name: Reason
fields.doc: Final result of the job

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Message about the current/final status

fields.name: ProgressBars
fields.doc: Information about the current job progress

fields.name: LastScheduleTime
fields.doc: Information when was the last time the job was successfully scheduled.

fields.name: CompleteTime
fields.doc: Information when the job's state changes to Complete.

fields.name: Results
fields.doc: Evaluation results

api_types.name: LMEvalJob
api_types.doc: LMEvalJob is the Schema for the lmevaljobs API

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Unitxt card's ID

fields.name: Custom
fields.doc: A JSON string for a custom unitxt card which contains the custom dataset. Use the documentation here: https://www.unitxt.ai/en/latest/docs/adding_dataset.html#adding-to-the-catalog to compose a custom card, store it as a JSON file, and use the JSON content as the value here.

api_types.name: LMEvalJobList
api_types.doc: LMEvalJobList contains a list of LMEvalJob

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Unitxt template ID

fields.name: Ref
fields.doc: The name of the custom template in the custom field. Its value is a JSON string for a custom Unitxt template. Use the documentation here: https://www.unitxt.ai/en/latest/docs/adding_template.html to compose a custom template, store it as a JSON file by calling the add_to_catalog API: https://www.unitxt.ai/en/latest/docs/saving_and_loading_from_catalog.html#adding-assets-to-the-catalog, and use the JSON content as the value here.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Unitxt System Prompt id

fields.name: Ref
fields.doc: The name of the custom systemPrompt in the custom field. Its value is a custom system prompt string

api_types.name: NemoConfig
api_types.doc: NemoConfig holds information related to a single configuration of the NeMo Guardrails server

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name sets the id of this particular config within the NeMo Guardrails server. This will create a directory called /app/config/$Name. Since it $Name will be used a directory, it must only contain alphanumeric characters, dashes, and underscores.

fields.name: ConfigMaps
fields.doc: ConfigMaps is a list of configmaps that comprise the configuration. All files from these configmaps will be mounted within /app/config/$Name

fields.name: Default
fields.doc: The Default flag determines whether config is treated as the default config for the nemo-server. If no config is set to default, the first entry in NemoConfigs will be used as the default

api_types.name: NemoGuardrailsSpec
api_types.doc: NemoGuardrailsSpec defines the desired state of NemoGuardrails

fields.name: NemoConfigs
fields.doc: NemoConfig should be the names of the configmaps containing NeMO server configuration files. All files in NemoConfigs will be mounted to /app/config/$Name

fields.name: Replicas
fields.doc: Number of replicas for the NeMo Guardrails deployment

fields.name: Env
fields.doc: Define Env information for the main container

api_types.name: NemoGuardrailStatus
api_types.doc: NemoGuardrailStatus defines the observed state of NemoGuardrails

fields.name: Conditions
fields.doc: Conditions describes the state of the NemoGuardrails resource.

fields.name: CA
fields.doc: CA describes the status of the CA configmaps

api_types.name: NemoGuardrails
api_types.doc: NemoGuardrails is the Schema for the nemoguardrails API

api_types.name: NemoGuardrailsList
api_types.doc: NemoGuardrailsList contains a list of NemoGuardrails

api_types.name: TrustyAIService
api_types.doc: TrustyAIService is the Schema for the trustyaiservices API

fields.name: Format
fields.doc: Format only supports "PVC" or "DATABASE" values

api_types.name: TrustyAIServiceSpec
api_types.doc: TrustyAIServiceSpec defines the desired state of TrustyAIService

fields.name: Replicas
fields.doc: Number of replicas

api_types.name: TrustyAIServiceStatus
api_types.doc: TrustyAIServiceStatus defines the observed state of TrustyAIService

fields.name: Phase
fields.doc: Define your status fields here

api_types.name: TrustyAIServiceList
api_types.doc: TrustyAIServiceList contains a list of TrustyAIService

api_types.name: TrustyAIService
api_types.doc: TrustyAIService is the Schema for the trustyaiservices API

fields.name: Format
fields.doc: Format only supports "PVC" or "DATABASE" values

api_types.name: TrustyAIServiceSpec
api_types.doc: TrustyAIServiceSpec defines the desired state of TrustyAIService

fields.name: Replicas
fields.doc: Number of replicas

api_types.name: TrustyAIServiceStatus
api_types.doc: TrustyAIServiceStatus defines the observed state of TrustyAIService

fields.name: Phase
fields.doc: Define your status fields here

api_types.name: Condition
api_types.doc: Condition represents possible conditions of a TrustyAIServiceStatus

api_types.name: TrustyAIServiceList
api_types.doc: TrustyAIServiceList contains a list of TrustyAIService

operator_config.name: AllTasServices
operator_config.doc: convenient list to store all registered services

operator_config.name: TasServices
operator_config.doc: to store all controllers and their set up function

operator_config.name: DSCConfigMapName
operator_config.doc: DSCConfigMapName is the name of the ConfigMap created by OpenDataHub operator

operator_config.name: DSCPermitOnlineKey
operator_config.doc: DSC Configuration keys

operator_config.name: ServiceName
operator_config.doc: Service name for registration

operator_config.name: allowPrivilegeEscalation
operator_config.doc: Default security context

operator_config.name: collectionLabel
operator_config.doc: Collection ConfigMap configuration

operator_config.name: configDirPath
operator_config.doc: EvalHub config directory (contains config.yaml and providers/ subdir)

operator_config.name: configMapName
operator_config.doc: Configuration constants

operator_config.name: containerName
operator_config.doc: Container configuration

operator_config.name: dbSecretVolumeName
operator_config.doc: Database configuration

operator_config.name: defaultEvalHubImage
operator_config.doc: Default image configuration

operator_config.name: mlflowTokenVolumeName
operator_config.doc: MLFlow projected token configuration

operator_config.name: providerLabel
operator_config.doc: Provider ConfigMap configuration

operator_config.name: routeName
operator_config.doc: Route configuration

operator_config.name: runAsNonRootUser
operator_config.doc: Default pod security context

operator_config.name: serviceCAVolumeName
operator_config.doc: Service CA configuration

operator_config.name: serviceName
operator_config.doc: Service configuration

operator_config.name: sidecarBaseURL
operator_config.doc: Sidecar configuration

operator_config.name: tlsSecretMountPath
operator_config.doc: TLS configuration (OpenShift service serving certificates)

operator_config.name: annotationFailurePending
operator_config.doc: annotationFailurePending: in-flight until POST succeeds and we set annotationFailureReported.

operator_config.name: evalHubEvaluationJobFailureControllerName
operator_config.doc: evalHubEvaluationJobFailureControllerName matches ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).Named(...) for logs and registration.

operator_config.name: evalHubExtraCABundleEnv
operator_config.doc: evalHubExtraCABundleEnv, if set, is a filesystem path to additional PEM CA certs (optional).

operator_config.name: evalHubInstanceNameLabel
operator_config.doc: EvalHub CR identity for getEvalHubURLFromJob (set by eval-hub job_builders; must match eval-hub).

operator_config.name: openshiftServiceCAMountPath
operator_config.doc:
openshiftServiceCAMountPath: PEM for the OpenShift service signing CA (trust in-cluster *.svc HTTPS, e.g. EvalHub).
Appended to the HTTP client root CAs; the in-cluster SA transport defaults to apiserver trust only.

operator_config.name: authReviewerClusterRoleName
operator_config.doc:
authReviewerClusterRoleName is the ClusterRole for EvalHub SAR auth checks only.
It contains only tokenreviews/create and subjectaccessreviews/create permissions.

operator_config.name: mlflowAccessClusterRoleName
operator_config.doc:
MLFlow access uses custom ClusterRoles scoped to the "mlflow.kubeflow.org" API group.
MLFlow's kubernetes-workspace-provider checks permissions via SelfSubjectAccessReview
against this group (not core Kubernetes resources). The ClusterRoles are pre-created
at operator installation time (config/rbac/evalhub_mlflow_access_role.yaml and
config/rbac/evalhub_mlflow_jobs_role.yaml).

operator_config.name: mlflowJobsAccessClusterRoleName
operator_config.doc:
mlflowJobsAccessClusterRoleName is a restricted MLflow ClusterRole for job pods.
Jobs only need create, get, list -- not update or delete.

operator_config.name: tenantLabel
operator_config.doc:
tenantLabel is the label that marks a namespace as an EvalHub tenant namespace.
When the operator detects a namespace with this label, it automatically provisions
the job ServiceAccount and RBAC bindings required for evaluation jobs to run there.

operator_config.name: gatewayBuiltInDetector
operator_config.value:
- name: %s
    input: true
    output: true
    detector_params:
      regex:
        - $^

operator_config.name: gatewayExternalDetector
operator_config.value:
- name: %s
    input: true
    output: true
    detector_params: {}

operator_config.name: gatewayPreamable
operator_config.doc: === gateway config constants ========

operator_config.name: orchestratorBuiltInConfig
operator_config.value:
%s:
    type: text_contents
    service:
      hostname: 127.0.0.1
      port: 8080
    chunker_id: whole_doc_chunker
    default_threshold: 0.5

operator_config.name: orchestratorConfigPreamble
operator_config.doc: === orchestrator config constants ========

operator_config.name: orchestratorConfigPreambleWithTLS
operator_config.value:
chat_generation:
  service:
    hostname: %s
    port: %s
    tls: %s-tls
detectors:

operator_config.name: orchestratorExternalDetector
operator_config.value:
%s:
    type: text_contents
    service:
      hostname: %q
      port: %s
    chunker_id: whole_doc_chunker
    default_threshold: 0.5

operator_config.name: orchestratorExternalDetectorWithTLS
operator_config.value:
%s:
    type: text_contents
    service:
      hostname: %q
      port: %s
      tls: %s-tls
    chunker_id: whole_doc_chunker
    default_threshold: 0.5

operator_config.name: DefaultPort
operator_config.doc: the default port for the driver to listen on

operator_config.name: progressMsgPattern
operator_config.doc: from https://gist.github.com/andenacitelli/20a98f8c45499fe21d55266b776d3071 -> regex pattern to extract tqdm fields

operator_config.name: pullPolicyMap
AllowedModels
operator_config.doc: AllowedModels allows for a subset of model types

operator_config.name: PatternArgName
operator_config.doc: PatternArgName allows for alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, and dots in argument names

operator_config.name: PatternArgValue
operator_config.doc: PatternArgValue allows for reasonable characters (including spaces) in arg values

operator_config.name: PatternLimit
operator_config.doc: PatternLimit allows for integers, floats, and percentage in limit values

operator_config.name: PatternModelName
operator_config.doc: PatternModelName allows for alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, forward slashes, dots, and colons in model names

operator_config.name: PatternTaskName
operator_config.doc: PatternTaskName allows for alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, parentheses in task names

operator_config.name: PatternTemplateName
operator_config.doc: PatternTemplateName allows for alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, dots, and colons in template names

operator_config.name: maxConfigMapKeyLength
operator_config.doc:
maxConfigMapKeyLength defines the maximum allowed length for ConfigMap keys
based on Kubernetes DNS subdomain name limits.

operator_config.name: validConfigMapKeyRegex
operator_config.doc:
validConfigMapKeyRegex defines allowed characters for ConfigMap keys.
Kubernetes ConfigMap keys must be valid DNS subdomain names or data keys.
